metadata.challenge_summary: The agents must choose an ordering across combat, hazard, and restraint objectives; the best order depends on party state.
dilemmas: Courage path is direct but damaging
Restraint path sacrifices treasure but lowers pressure
warden_runbook: Adapt pressure to chosen proof order
Do not make combat always optimal
metadata.lite_focus: A four-room proof sampler: one hazard, one combat threat, one optional restraint object, and a final ember token.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: choose proof order and preserve resources for extraction
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Trial of Embers is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

upper_right.name: Upper Right
upper_right.description: A compact pressure room isolates the challenge: multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies.

lower_left.name: Lower Left
lower_left.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

lower_right.name: Lower Right
lower_right.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Care Bowl
furniture.description: Primary clue object for multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

furniture.name: Support Marker
furniture.description: A small support object that makes the non-damaging coordination action visible in transcript review.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A four-room proof sampler: one hazard, one combat threat, one optional restraint object, and a final ember token.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Adapt pressure to chosen proof order
Do not make combat always optimal
pressure the declared MARL axis: multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.

crypt_brute_1.name: Trial Brute
crypt_brute_1.public_summary: Lite boss for multi-objective sequencing with non-dominant strategies; it should test coordination before raw damage.
crypt_brute_1.counterplay_hint: Search the focus object and coordinate roles before committing to damage.
crypt_brute_1.defeat_description: Defeat Trial Brute after using the lite challenge's coordination lesson.
